title: Value ledger records recall, stale-withheld, and caller-answered receipts
summary: Kage persists a per repo value ledger at .agent memory/reports/value.json schema version 1, events capped at 5000 with all time totals that survive trimming, temp file + rename writes . recallWithVectorScores in mcp/kern
body:
Kage persists a per-repo value ledger at .agent_memory/reports/value.json (schema_version 1, events capped at 5000 with all-time totals that survive trimming, temp-file + rename writes). recallWithVectorScores in mcp/kernel.ts records recall_served (tokens_saved = cited source bytes/4 minus context block length/4, floored at 0) and one stale_withheld event per hard-stale packet suppressed; queryCodeGraph records caller_answered when a caller-intent query is answered from the call-edge index, guarded on .agent_memory existing so bare code-graph queries stay read-only. valueSummary() exposes today/last_7d/all_time windows with a $15-per-1M-token dollar estimate; receipts surface via 'kage gains', a receipt line after 'kage recall', and a Gains line appended to the kage_context MCP handler in mcp/index.ts.
Evidence: mcp/kernel.ts recordValueEvent/valueSummary; tests: kernel.test.ts value ledger tests pass (193 total)
